channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Aluminum Falls on Likely US Tariff Slash
item.description: Aluminum futures in the UK fell to $3,050 per tonne, continuing the pullback from a three-year high. The decline follows reports of a likely US tariff reduction on aluminum imports.

item.title: February 14: Aluminum Prices Strong While Downstream Orders Remain Weak; Aluminum Billet Processing Fees in Three Regions Remain Flat Compared to Yesterday - Daily Review of Spot Aluminum Billets
item.description: Aluminum prices remained strong on February 14 despite weak downstream orders. Aluminum billet processing fees in three regions stayed flat compared to the previous day.

item.title: Aluminum price rises as looming smelter closure adds to supply strains
item.description: South32 Ltd. is proceeding with plans to mothball its Mozal smelter in Mozambique next month, a major supplier to the European market. The closure adds to growing supply constraints as Chinese smelters near government-imposed production capacity caps and 50% US tariffs have caused imports to slump, with aluminum prices rallying 17% last year and 5.5% so far in 2026.

item.title: Bessent discusses possible narrowing of steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent commented on reports of potential tariff rollbacks, stating that any changes would likely involve clarification on incidental objects rather than broad scaling back. The Trump administration has imposed 50% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports, with discussions ongoing about possible modifications.

item.title: Bessent discusses possible narrowing of steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent discussed the Trump administration's potential plan to scale back 50 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum imports to address rising affordability issues. Officials believe the tariffs are hurting consumers by raising prices for goods including pie tins and food-and-drink cans.

item.title: Trump plans to roll back some tariffs on steel, aluminium goods
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump plans to scale back some tariffs on steel and aluminum goods, according to Financial Times reporting. The move comes as Commerce Department and trade officials believe the tariffs are hurting consumers, with shares of major steelmakers declining following the announcement.

item.title: Bessent discusses possible narrowing of steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: US Treasury Secretary Bessent discussed potential narrowing of 50% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports and derivatives like appliances in a CNBC interview. The Trump administration imposed these tariffs, but changes may clarify incidental items ahead of midterm elections. He noted it would be the president's decision after consulting the US Trade Rep.

item.title: Nickel Futures Prices Inched Lower in February
item.description: Nickel prices declined in February amid macroeconomic headwinds including higher-than-expected CPI (6.4%) and PPI (6%) data, along with hawkish Federal Reserve commentary raising interest rate concerns. The LME announced on February 23 that Asian nickel trading would resume on March 20, which also contributed to price declines.

item.title: Philippine nickel sales to Indonesia to jump after Jakarta curbs
item.description: Philippine nickel ore shipments to Indonesia could double to 30 million tons in 2026 following Indonesia's production cuts. Philippine miners see this as an opportunity to fill the supply gap created by Indonesia's domestic output reduction, though expansion may be limited by aging mines and slow approval processes.

item.title: Aluminum pulls back after a one-week low following market digests reporting Trump may ease tariffs
item.description: Aluminum prices fell to their lowest level in a week on Friday after reports that the U.S. may slash some import tariffs. The benchmark three-month aluminum on the LME lost as much as 2.7% but reduced losses to 0.9% by end of trading at $3,074/t.

item.title: Aluminum pulls back after a one-week low following market digests reporting Trump may ease tariffs
item.description: Aluminum prices fell to a one-week low on Friday, dropping as much as 2.7% to $3,074 per metric ton on the LME after reports of potential U.S. tariff reductions. The metal had peaked due to supply concerns but pulled back amid tariff speculation. Alcoa and Century Aluminum shares declined alongside steelmakers.

item.title: EU steelmakers demand electricity price cut to safeguard the bloc's competitiveness
item.description: European steelmakers are urging the EU to reduce electricity prices to around €50/MWh to protect the bloc's steel industry competitiveness. The demand addresses high energy costs impacting production viability amid global challenges.

item.title: Nornickel, a Russian nickel producer, will shift some of its nickel production to a copper plant for repairs
item.description: Nornickel plans to maintain 2026 nickel production at 193,000-203,000 metric tonnes by shifting some output to a copper facility during a 270-day smelting furnace upgrade at Nadezhda Metallurgical Plant in 2027. This unconventional approach offsets half the plant's capacity loss. The company is preparing production lines for pilot tests this year.

item.title: Trump to Roll Back Some Tariffs on Steel, Aluminum
item.description: Aluminum prices sank to a one-week low after reports that Trump may trim import tariffs, with LME's three-month contract slipping over 1.18% to $3,063.50 a ton and Shanghai's most-active contract falling 1.76% to 23,195 yuan a ton. Trading slowed in China ahead of the Lunar New Year break from February 15 to 24. Copper prices also tumbled amid these pressures.

item.title: Trump Moves to Roll Back Metal & Aluminum Tariffs as Affordability Pressures Mount
item.description: The Trump administration plans targeted easing of metal tariffs ahead of 2026 midterms to address affordability for voters and manufacturers. This follows earlier bolstering from Iran tensions but recent drops on deal prospects, weakening demand, and supply growth per IEA forecasts. Prices show slowing downside amid persistent oversupply concerns.

item.title: Aluminium drops on report Trump may ease tariffs
item.description: Aluminium prices fell to a one-week low on reports of potential US tariff trims, with other industrial metals also declining. Trading volumes were low ahead of holidays, contributing to the price drop.

item.title: Trump to Roll Back Some Tariffs on Steel, Aluminum
item.description: US President Trump plans to scale back some steel and aluminum tariffs due to concerns over rising consumer prices for goods like cans and pie tins. Aluminum prices dropped to a one-week low following the report, with trading slowed in China due to Lunar New Year closure. The administration will exempt certain items and pursue targeted security probes instead of broad expansions.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Steel prices rose to 3,056 CNY/T on February 13, 2026, up 0.53% from the previous day, though down 2.80% over the past month and 4.68% year-over-year. Recent news highlights steel rebar futures falling to a one-month low around CNY 3,070/ton on February 9 due to mills trimming production ahead of Lunar New Year. Earlier in February, prices pulled back from five-month highs amid declining industrial metals.

item.title: Mid-February 2026 Steel Pipe Market: Navigating the Final Stretch of the Holiday Hiatus
item.description: Steel pipe production remains idle but is expected to resume in a staggered manner from the week of February 23rd onwards, with full stable output anticipated by early March. The supply chain is preparing for reactivation, with initial post-holiday lead times potentially extending to 50-65 days as manufacturers clear significant order backlogs from before the holiday shutdown.

item.title: Copper Cables: Antitrust Launches Investigation into Alleged Cartel
item.description: Italy's Antitrust Authority has initiated an investigation into major low-voltage copper cable manufacturers, including Prysmian Cavi e Sistemi Italia Srl, for alleged price-fixing since 2005. Inspections were conducted yesterday at company offices, distributors, and the National Federation of Electrical Material Wholesalers, supported by Guardia di Finanza. Prysmian shares dropped 1.26% in response, though analysts view the impact as negligible given the small market share.

item.title: Steel industry faces challenges amid domestic focus shift
item.description: Vietnamese steel companies are shifting focus to the domestic market due to weakening export demand and trade barriers, leading to intense competition and compressed profit margins. Companies like Nam Kim Steel are expanding production capacity with new plants such as the Phú Mỹ Steel plant set to start in Q1 2026, but simultaneous expansions by competitors like VNSteel and Dong A Steel risk oversupply. Analysts highlight challenges in achieving supply-demand balance and rising operating costs impacting profitability.

item.title: Why copper and nickel prices are moving today: key market drivers - Feb 15, 2026
item.description: Copper prices rose to $5.80/lb (+0.30%) as of the latest update on Feb 13, 2026, driven by supply tightness, electrification demand, stockpiling, policy risks, supply disruptions, and China demand signals. The market remains sensitive to inventory flows and tariff uncertainties. Nickel prices fell to $16,995/tonne (-1.48%) amid Indonesia supply issues.

item.title: Why copper and nickel prices are moving today: key market drivers - Feb 15, 2026
item.description: Nickel prices are modestly lower at $16,995/tonne as of the February 13 close, amid choppy trading balancing Indonesia-driven supply headlines against oversupply concerns. The market remains sensitive to stainless steel demand and global growth expectations. Copper holds near elevated levels, but nickel faces demand-cycle uncertainty.

item.title: Why copper and nickel prices are moving today: key market drivers - Feb 15, 2026
item.description: Nickel prices reacted to Indonesia ordering Weda Bay, the world's biggest nickel mine complex, to cut its 2026 ore quota. As of February 13 close, nickel stood at $16,995/ton amid supply management headlines. This contributes to broader market movements influenced by Indonesian supply dynamics.

item.title: LME aluminium cash offer price drops 3.88% to $2,996/t; opening stock slips 0.41%
item.description: LME aluminium prices recorded a sharp decline on February 13, with cash bid prices falling to $2,995/t from $3,116.5/t (3.90% decline). The price drop is attributed to stock market declines, profit-taking, position squaring, strong US labour data, and reduced China demand before Lunar New Year.

item.title: White House denies reports of steel, aluminum tariff rollback
item.description: The White House pushed back against recent reports suggesting a potential rollback of the 50% steel and aluminum tariffs. White House trade advisor Peter Navarro stated there was no basis for reports that the administration planned to reduce these tariffs.

item.title: White House denies reports of steel, aluminum tariff rollback
item.description: The White House denied reports suggesting a potential rollback of the 50% steel and aluminum tariffs. This statement addresses market speculation impacting commodity prices and related stocks.

item.title: Aluminum Cans Recycling Services Market Projected to Reach
item.description: Rising primary aluminum costs are making recycled aluminum economically attractive, as recycling consumes 95% less energy than primary production. Technological advancements including AI-powered sorting systems and advanced metallurgical analysis are expanding market opportunities, with Asia-Pacific capturing 35% of incremental growth and mandatory recycling targets driving $4.8 billion in infrastructure investment.

item.title: Brazil to Host Latin America's First Exclusive Metal Packaging Congress
item.description: Brazil is hosting Latin America's first exclusive metal packaging congress. Limited details are available from the press release provided.

item.title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: HRC Steel fell to 965.04 USD/T on February 16, 2026, down 0.72% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 2.89%, with a yearly increase of 26.56%. The data was last updated on February 15, 2026.

item.title: February 16, 2026 Scrap & Used Car Market Update
item.description: Ferrous scrap shows modest improvement, with shredded steel trading in the $290-$315 per ton range. Mills are returning from the holiday with restocked order books. This indicates positive trends in the scrap steel market.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 16.02.2026
item.description: SteelRadar provides real-time updates on global iron and steel prices, including Shanghai Futures rebar terms as of February 13, 2026. Various futures contracts for regions like China, Turkey, Europe, and North America are listed. The platform offers analyses and comments on the industry.

item.title: White House denies reports of steel, aluminum tariff rollback
item.description: The White House has denied recent reports suggesting a potential rollback of the 50% tariffs on steel and aluminum. This stance maintains current trade protections amid ongoing commodity market discussions.

item.title: Copper Pricing
item.description: International Wire reports the copper base price at $8.4000 effective February 16, 2026, for Omega - Camden. Additional indices include COMEX copper monthly average at $5.88100 and LME copper cash settlement at $5.9370 for January 2026. This provides a current pricing snapshot amid ongoing market trends.

item.title: Global copper exchange stocks top 1Mt for first time in two decades
item.description: Copper stocks on the world's three biggest metal exchanges exceeded one-million metric tons for the first time in more than two decades. This buildup signals shifting market dynamics potentially impacting prices and supply trends.

item.title: Global copper exchange stocks top 1Mt for first time in two decades
item.description: Copper stocks on the world's three biggest metal exchanges have exceeded one-million metric tons for the first time in more than two decades. This buildup indicates shifting supply dynamics amid recent market pressures. The increase highlights potential easing of immediate tightness despite ongoing production challenges.

item.title: The Monday Report - 16 February 2026
item.description: Spot nickel prices fell 5.10% to $7.63/lb in the latest futures update. The report covers broader metals market movements, including gains in gold and silver amid expectations for UK CPI data. Energy futures show minor declines in crude oil prices.

item.title: Copper Values Retreat to $5.9 Per Pound as Chinese Industrial Engine Idles for Lunar New Year
item.description: Copper futures settled near $5.90 per pound due to a seasonal slowdown in China during Lunar New Year starting February 17, 2026, leading to reduced industrial activity and inventory buildup. This follows volatility with peaks near $6.50 in late January. Post-holiday recovery in March could see range-bound trading between $5.75 and $6.10, with potential rallies if stimulus boosts demand.

item.title: BHP Earnings Feb. 17: 62C Dividend Seen, Copper Guide Raised
item.description: BHP has lifted its FY26 copper production guidance to 1,900–2,000 kt ahead of its half-year results on February 17, 2026. The company highlights strong copper performance and resilience in China's commodity demand. This guidance uplift supports valuation and dividend confidence amid favorable prices.

item.title: Norilsk Nickel will be the first in the world to launch a unique tunneling complex in the Arctic
item.description: Norilsk Nickel plans to test its unique 700-ton Norda mechanized tunneling complex at the Taimyrsky mine in Q2 2026, targeting 150,000 tons of ore production that year from the Severnaya-4 deposit. The equipment promises record penetration speeds and improved safety. All specialists are trained, and assembly is underway.

item.title: U.S., Nippon Steel to reline Gary Works blast furnace this year
item.description: U.S. Steel and Nippon Steel plan to reline blast furnace #14 at Gary Works starting May 2026, costing $350 million and lasting 100 days until August, ensuring no supply interruptions. The company will invest $900 million in 2026, followed by $800 million in 2027 and $1.1 billion in 2028. Local activists oppose the project over environmental concerns, advocating for greener steelmaking.

item.title: U.S., Nippon Steel to reline Gary Works blast furnace this year
item.description: U.S. Steel and Nippon Steel plan to reline blast furnace #14 at the Gary Works facility later this year as part of a $900 million investment in 2026, followed by further investments in 2027 and 2028. The project aims to implement high-productivity, energy-efficient technologies to reduce CO2 emissions intensity. This follows board approval in December 2025 for the $350 million reline of the facility's largest blast furnace.

item.title: BHP Boosts Profit Amid Copper Surge
item.description: BHP reported a 28% profit surge to US$5.64 billion in the first half, driven by increased copper earnings and strong margins, allowing it to raise full-year 2026 copper production guidance to 1.9-2 million tonnes. Output from the Escondida mine and other Chilean operations contributed to this revision. The company declared an interim dividend of US73¢ per share, up from the previous year.

item.title: Copper becomes BHP's biggest earnings driver
item.description: BHP has increased its group copper production guidance for the 2026 financial year to between 1.9-million and 2.0-million tonnes. Copper has become the company's largest earnings driver.

item.title: Copper Becomes BHP's Top Earnings Driver in 2026
item.description: Copper has become BHP's largest earnings driver by 2026, boosting earnings with high margins.

item.title: What's Behind the Copper Hype? A Fireside Conversation with BHP Americas President Brandon Craig
item.description: CSIS hosts a discussion on copper market trends, including tightening supply, declining ore grades, and geopolitical risks affecting energy, data centers, and defense technologies. BHP Americas President Brandon Craig joins to explore U.S. strategies for copper security, investment, and innovation in the value chain. The event underscores copper's role in technological leadership and economic resilience.

item.title: Nickel price jumps as Indonesia’s top mine cuts output
item.description: LME nickel prices rose 2% to $17,835/tonne after Indonesia slashed PT Weda Bay Nickel's 2026 ore quota to 12 million tonnes from 42 million in 2025, extending a 20% rally. Total national quotas are set at 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million in 2025, tightening global supply. Eramet plans to request a quota revision.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Defy Tariff Rollback Rumors as 'Project Vault' and Carbon Levies Reshape Global Supply
item.description: Aluminum prices hold above $3,100 per tonne despite rumors of US tariff rollbacks, driven by structural supply deficits and the US government's Project Vault initiative launched on February 2 with $10 billion for essential minerals. The US Midwest Premium hit a record $560 per tonne, with LME stocks near historic lows under 300,000 tonnes. Market focus shifts to government buying and off-take agreements amid physical scarcity.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Defy Tariff Rollback Rumors as 'Project Vault' and Carbon Levies Reshape Global Supply
item.description: The global aluminum market is being driven by the U.S. government's $12 billion 'Project Vault' initiative to build a Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve, which is supporting prices despite tariff rollback speculation. Critically low domestic inventories and the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism are reshaping valuations more than trade policy, with the Midwest Premium reaching a record $560 per tonne.

item.title: U.S. Raw Steel Production Climbs 5.6% Year Over Year in Mid-February 2026
item.description: U.S. domestic raw steel production reached 1.8 million net tons in the week ending February 14, 2026, with mills operating at 77.8% capability utilization. This represents a 5.6% increase compared to the same week in 2025, and year-to-date production through February 14 is up 4.0% to 11.357 million net tons. The Southern district led regional output with 826,000 net tons.

item.title: Metals market alert: Why copper price is falling today after stockpiles hit 20-year high
item.description: Copper prices fell as much as 0.8% below $12,800 a ton as LME inventories hit a 20-year high, with global stocks exceeding 1 million tons across major exchanges. In China, near-record prices cooled demand, leading to rebuilding inventories amid caution over U.S. tariffs. Aluminum and other metals also slipped as markets awaited tariff clarity.

item.title: LME aluminium cash offer eases to $3,014/t as opening stocks drop 0.42% to 477,550t on February 17
item.description: LME aluminium cash prices declined 0.77% to $3,014 per tonne on February 17, with opening stocks dropping 0.42% to 477,550 tonnes and cancelled warrants rising sharply. The three-month Asian Reference Price stood at $3,035 per tonne, while LME alumina Platts price was $306.93 per tonne. This reflects ongoing inventory adjustments amid market pressures.

item.title: Aluminum prices fall after report of curbs to tariffs
item.description: Aluminum prices in London slipped 1.7% to $3,045 per tonne following reports that the Trump administration plans to curb tariffs on some metal products to address surging consumer prices. The White House is reviewing affected products for exemptions and considering a targeted national security probe instead of broad expansions. Prices are down nearly 2% for the week but up 2% year-to-date.

item.title: Aluminum price drops as Trump moves to narrow levies on metal products
item.description: Aluminum prices fell up to 2.7% on the LME as traders reacted to reports of President Trump planning to narrow the scope of import tariffs on aluminum and steel due to consumer impacts. The metal settled 0.7% lower at $3,077.50 per tonne, with ample near-term supplies indicated by expanding price discounts between contracts. Treasury Secretary downplayed any major rollback.

item.title: LME aluminium cash offer eases to $3,014/t as opening stocks drop 0.42% to 477,550t on February 17
item.description: LME aluminum prices declined 0.77% on February 17, with cash offer prices falling to $3,014 per tonne. Warehouse stocks decreased slightly while cancelled warrants surged 23.66%, indicating shifting market dynamics in inventory management.

item.title: Conscious Choice―Shaping Mobility's Future Through Recycled Aluminum
item.description: AFEELA is incorporating high-purity, high-strength recycled aluminum into vehicle body panels and motor mounts through collaboration with Honda and suppliers. This approach ensures safety and performance via meticulous scrap selection, refining, and quality assurance. The initiative sets standards for sustainable mobility by integrating recycled materials into structural components.

item.title: Jindal Steel Ltd. Hits New 52-Week High of Rs.1230 on 18 Feb 2026
item.description: Jindal Steel Ltd. reached a new 52-week and all-time high of Rs.1230, reflecting a strong rally in the ferrous metals sector with 44.44% one-year appreciation. The stock trades above all major moving averages, indicating a well-established uptrend despite slight daily lag versus the sector. Its Mojo Grade is Hold, upgraded from Sell in November 2025.

item.title: SGH Steel Dynamics Makes A$15B BlueScope Takeover Bid
item.description: SGH Steel Dynamics has made a A$15 billion takeover offer for BlueScope, representing significant consolidation activity in the global steel market. This cross-border deal reflects ongoing industry consolidation trends in 2026.

item.title: Conscious Choice―Shaping Mobility's Future Through Recycled Materials
item.description: Sony Honda Mobility's AFEELA vehicle incorporates recycled steel and aluminum into structural components including body panels, motor mounts, and load-bearing parts. The company has developed an integrated system for material sourcing and quality assurance that enables recycled materials to meet performance standards comparable to virgin materials, setting new standards for sustainable vehicle development.

item.title: Teck Announces 2025 Production and Sales Update and Reaffirms Outlook
item.description: Teck reports 2025 annual copper production of 453,500 tonnes, meeting guidance, with strong Q4 performance across assets including 55,400 tonnes from Quebrada Blanca. Copper sales in Q4 were lower than production due to inventory buildup from weather delays. The company reaffirms 2026 copper production guidance for Antamina at 95,000–105,000 tonnes with no changes to other 2026–2028 guidance.

item.title: Why a tighter Copper Market puts Southern Hemisphere Mining front and centre – Investor Stream First Look
item.description: The article discusses how a tightening copper market positions Southern Hemisphere Mining prominently for investors. It highlights recent drilling results summary from the Llahuin project as featured in a video. This comes amid broader market dynamics emphasizing copper supply constraints.

item.title: LME Nickel Futures Drop 0.93% on February 18
item.description: On February 18, London Metal Exchange (LME) nickel futures prices fell by 0.93%. This decline reflects recent market volatility influenced by Indonesian supply dynamics and global demand trends. The drop adds to ongoing price swings in the nickel market.

item.title: Nickel Industries lifts 2026 quota, driving stronger outlook and margins
item.description: Nickel Industries received a 60% increase in its 2026 RKAB nickel ore sales quota to 14.3 million wet metric tonnes, boosting production capacity and margins. January 2026 saw Adjusted EBITDA of about US$50 million, driven by higher nickel prices and improved NPI margins up 150% to US$2,800/t. The quota supports stable supply for RKEF and HPAL operations amid positive market trends.

item.title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Falls Toward US$16,875/Ton (Tuesday, February 17, 2026)
item.description: The 3-month nickel commodity price declined to around US$16,875 per ton as of February 17, 2026. This drop signals short-term bearish pressure in the nickel market. It follows broader trends of volatility tied to supply and demand factors.

item.title: LME nickel prices recover to $18000 as market confidence stabilizes
item.description: LME nickel prices rebounded to US$18,000 per ton, stabilizing market confidence after an earlier sharp drop. This recovery provides relief to the stainless steel sector, which relies heavily on nickel. It reflects improving sentiment amid supply constraints and demand recovery.

item.title: Nickel Industries lifts 2026 quota, driving stronger outlook and margins
item.description: Nickel Industries received a 60% increase in its 2026 RKAB nickel ore sales quota to 14.3 million wet metric tonnes, up from 9.0 million wmt. The company reported January 2026 Adjusted EBITDA from Operations of approximately US$50 million, with significantly improved margins across its nickel pig iron and HPAL operations.

item.title: Nickel Industries secures 60% increase in 2026 ore sales quota
item.description: Nickel miner and processor Nickel Industries Ltd announced a 60% increase in its 2026 ore sales quota. This development reflects the company's strengthened position following Indonesia's updated production quotas for the year.

item.title: Indonesia tightens control on nickel as US and China scramble for critical minerals
item.description: Indonesia, which controls approximately 60% of global nickel supply as of 2024, is implementing stricter regulatory controls on nickel mining and processing. The crackdown includes seizures of mining operations and significant fines, though analysts note that nickel's strategic importance may be declining as Chinese EV manufacturers shift to battery chemistries requiring less nickel.

item.title: Tenaris Acquires isOplus Oil and Gas Division in $10M Deal
item.description: Tenaris acquired isOplus's oil and gas division for $10 million, gaining ownership of pre-insulated pipeline systems and anti-corrosion coatings. The business unit was rebranded as Tenaris Coating Italy and will apply anti-corrosion coatings to line pipes at Tenaris facilities across Italy, Mexico, Argentina, and Brazil.

item.title: Tenaris to Launch TenarisShawcor White Jacket™ Coating at the Hydrogen World Expo
item.description: Tenaris unveiled TenarisShawcor White Jacket™, an advanced coating solution developed at its Villamarzana, Italy facility for severe onshore and underground pipeline applications. The coating is suitable for hydrogen and carbon capture transportation pipelines, offering enhanced resistance to material embrittlement.

item.title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Innovation Awards to Spotlight Industry Breakthroughs
item.description: ALUMINIUM 2026 has launched new industry innovation awards highlighting breakthroughs in Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing. Exhibitors can submit entries from May, with winners announced at the event after industry voting. The awards aim to celebrate and propagate innovations driving environmental impact reduction and efficiency.

item.title: Global aluminum market braces for supply disruptions, volatility and margin squeeze
item.description: Middle East tensions, including Maersk suspending Strait of Hormuz crossings, threaten 9% of global aluminum production, likely causing smelter slowdowns at sites like Alba in Bahrain. Traders anticipate price rises for aluminum products. Rotterdam premiums increased week-over-week amid these concerns.

item.title: Indonesia Shakes Global Nickel Market: World's Largest Mine Sees 70% Production Cut in Push to Inflate Prices
item.description: Indonesia's ESDM has capped PT Weda Bay Nickel's 2026 output at 12 million wet metric tonnes, a 71% cut from 42 million in 2025, triggering a 2% surge in LME nickel prices toward $18,000 per tonne. This shift from surplus to potential shortage is forcing supply chain reassessments for battery and steel producers. The mine has applied for a quota revision, with possible imports from the Philippines if limits hold.

item.title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Futures Price Drops to US$17,130/Ton
item.description: The 3-month forward nickel commodity futures price fell 2.45% to US$17,130 per ton on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. Year-to-date trading has shown growth of 1.27%, with the highest monthly average recorded in January 2026 at US$18,030 per ton.

item.title: Steel & Aluminum Price Outlook – March 2026 Market Update
item.description: Aluminum prices are rising rapidly due to drying imports, tariffs, and global events, with no relief in sight. The update covers supply chain challenges, capacity constraints, and advice for contractors to use escalation clauses ahead of construction season. Midwest premiums have stabilized slightly but overall pricing pressure persists.

item.title: Supply fears lift LME aluminium to nearly four-year high
item.description: LME aluminum prices hit a nearly four-year high of $3,544 per metric ton due to Middle East conflict disrupting supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, which accounts for 9% of global production. Qatalum smelter shutdown and Aluminium Bahrain's force majeure exacerbated concerns. Shanghai futures also rose amid better Chinese inflation data.

item.title: Steel & Aluminum Price Outlook – March 2026 Market Update
item.description: This video update discusses rising aluminum prices due to drying imports, tariffs, and global events impacting supply in Q1 2026. Experts cover steel mill output, the need for escalation clauses, and preparations for construction season. Aluminum market faces fast-moving price increases.

item.title: Steel & Aluminum Price Outlook – March 2026 Market Update
item.description: Steel continues to show rising mill output and strong demand, with prices expected to increase for the next 2-3 months. The update covers supply chain challenges, tariff impacts, and what contractors should expect as construction season ramps up.

item.title: Blog: The 11% question: women, talent, and sustainable steel
item.description: A blog post by Soo Jung Kim, Head of People Sustainability, explores the role of talent and workforce diversity in advancing sustainable steel production. The article reflects on over 25 years of industry experience and emphasizes that the steel sector continues to evolve.

item.title: Scrap Metal Market Recap | Aluminum 4¢ Copper 2¢
item.description: Aluminum led the scrap metal market with EC wire rising 4 cents per pound, while copper moved higher by 2 cents on the ScrapMonster Price Index. Brass, stainless, and steel prices remained unchanged during the period.

item.title: Copper theft ticks up as prices surge
item.description: Copper theft is increasing in South Florida amid surging copper prices. The report describes an 'evolution' in theft patterns linked to market trends. This highlights secondary market impacts of rising prices.

item.title: Aluminum prices surge to a four-year high amid war
item.description: Aluminum prices have surged to a four-year high amid ongoing war-related disruptions. The video discusses the rapid market movements and their implications.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News
item.description: Market update covering global steel industry news, pricing data, and analyses. Recent developments include Koç Group's partnership with Swedish green steel producer StegraİR and Nucor raising HRC prices by $5/st.

item.title: Copper Prices Jump 2% Today, After Hitting a Record High in Jan 2026
item.description: Copper prices jumped 2% on March 10, 2026, following a record high in January 2026 amid a projected 15-year global supply shortage. Existing supply constraints are keeping prices elevated, pressuring margins for cables and wires companies.

item.title: Copper price edges lower as traders await return of China demand
item.description: Copper prices dipped 0.1% to $13,304.50/t on the LME as traders await post-Lunar New Year demand rebound in China, with inventories surging to 531,700 tons at major centers, the highest since early 2020. Global exchange stockpiles are also rising amid tepid physical demand despite record highs driven by US trade policies and mine issues. Long-term bullishness persists due to supply constraints and rising demand from EVs, data centers, and renewables.

item.title: Why copper's pause may be more about policy than demand
item.description: Copper prices have cooled after rallying above $13,000 per tonne, with trade policy, US tariff risks, and shifting Chinese demand emerging as key factors shaping market direction. The consolidation phase reflects complex forces beyond electrification demand, including AI-driven energy infrastructure pressures.

item.title: LME nickel on Mar 10 gains slightly as geopolitical concerns ease, China economic indicators improve
item.description: The London Metal Exchange (LME) nickel futures price on March 10 slightly increased by US$19/ton to US$17,488/ton, with spot prices also edging up by US$14/ton. The modest gains were driven by easing geopolitical concerns and improving Chinese economic indicators.

item.title: Tariffs threaten Canadian aluminum critical to U.S. energy transition
item.description: Trump's announced doubling of tariffs on Canadian aluminum, set for March 12, threatens U.S. supplies vital for solar, wind, and EV energy transition, where demand is surging. Canadian smelters use low-carbon hydroelectric power unlike U.S. fossil fuel-dependent ones, and funding cuts jeopardize a new 'Green Smelter' project aimed at doubling domestic production with 75% lower emissions.

item.title: Steel rose to 3,107 CNY/T on March 11, 2026
item.description: Steel prices reached 3,107 CNY/T, up 0.29% from the previous day, hovering near five-week highs. China renewed its pledge to support the steel sector by curbing excess capacity, with economic planners signaling orderly reductions in steel capacity that could lift prices and improve profit margins.

item.title: Mercuria to withdraw nearly 100,000t of aluminum from LME
item.description: Commodity trader Mercuria plans to withdraw nearly 100,000 tons of aluminum from LME warehouses in Port Klang, Malaysia, amid the Strait of Hormuz shutdown freezing Middle East shipments. Middle East producers like Alba and Qatalum face production reductions and force majeure, straining supplies in Europe and the US where physical premiums have soared.

item.title: Novelis Wins Award for 100% Recycled End-Of-Life Vehicle Scrap Product
item.description: Novelis Inc. received the Münchner Management Kolloquium (MMK) Award of Excellence 2026 for developing an aluminium sheet made from 100% end-of-life vehicle scrap for exterior car body applications. The award recognizes the company's innovation in sustainable automotive material development and demonstrates the technical feasibility of car-to-car recycling for high-quality aluminium applications, representing a significant step toward decarbonizing the automotive sector.

item.title: Tariffs, demand boost US steel outlook: Nucor
item.description: Nucor reports historic order backlogs entering 2026, up nearly 40% annually in steel mills and 15% in products, supported by tariffs and demand. The company highlights ongoing issues like transshipping via Mexico and Canada under USMCA renegotiations and limited improvement in automotive and construction sectors. Overall demand is expected to rise slightly in 2026.

item.title: Major steel and raw material futures prices in China - March 10, 2026
item.description: SteelOrbis provides closing prices for major steel futures in China on March 10, 2026, including rebar at RMB 3,104/mt ($450/mt). Recent updates note rising longs prices in Turkey and Saudi Arabia amid demand, with weekly changes showing gains in rebar and HRC. The Orbis Steel Index indicates positive trends for Turkish rebar.

item.title: Aluminium extends gains as supply concerns amid Mideast conflict linger
item.description: Aluminum prices surged on Thursday due to persistent global supply tightening from the ongoing Middle East conflict. Disruptions continue to fuel concerns over production capacity and availability.

item.title: Green grant awarded: Advancing circular aluminium for sustainable water treatment
item.description: Alumichem has received a green grant to develop a circular innovation project that replaces virgin aluminium with recycled aluminium in aluminium chlorohydrate (ACH) production for drinking water and wastewater purification. The project, supported by Denmark's Environmental Technology Development and Demonstration Programme (MUDP) and conducted in collaboration with Stena Recycling and Stena Aluminium, aims to significantly reduce energy use, CO₂ emissions, and material waste while maintaining high quality standards for drinking water applications.

item.title: New steel and aluminum tariff rates take effect March 12, executive orders show
item.description: New 25% tariff rates on steel and aluminum imports into the United States are set to take effect on March 12, according to executive orders signed by President Donald Trump on Monday. A US official had previously indicated the rates would start on March 4, but the full texts of the orders were released hours after signing. These tariffs are expected to impact steel market dynamics significantly.

item.title: New steel and aluminum tariff rates take effect March 12, executive orders show
item.description: The new 25% tariffs on US steel and aluminum imports will take effect on March 12, as per executive orders signed by President Donald Trump. An earlier announcement suggested March 4, but the orders' full texts were delayed in release. This development is likely to influence steel pricing and trade flows.

item.title: Nickel Hits 19-month High
item.description: Nickel increased to 18,908.00 USD/T, the highest since June 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, nickel gained 19.58%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 21.08%.

item.title: Indonesia to cut nickel mining quota in 2026
item.description: Indonesia's energy ministry will reduce the 2026 nickel production quota to 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million tonnes in 2025 and below estimated consumption of 330 million tonnes. This cut aligns with market expectations but may lead to smelter production reductions or increased imports. The decision contributes to ongoing supply tightening in the nickel market.

item.title: Acciaierie d'Italia to start preferential negotiations with Azerbaijani consortium on acquisition of former Ilva plants
item.description: Extraordinary commissioners of Acciaierie d’Italia and ILVA in extraordinary administration have requested authorization from Italy’s Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy to begin preferential negotiations with an Azerbaijani consortium (Baku Steel Company CJSC and Azerbaijan Investment Company OJSC) for the sale of the former Ilva steel plants.[8] Ten proposals had been received by the January 10 deadline, and the chosen consortium was selected based on criteria including financial stability, industrial sustainability, and expected benefits for employment and local communities.[8]
